title: psacalium radulifolium
intro: Psacalium radulifolium, also known as the false yerba, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Central America, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Psacalium radulifolium include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat digestive problems, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Psacalium radulifolium typically involve drying and infusing the leaves, stems, or roots to create teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
